related_questions.question: How to reset an iPhone with an unresponsive screen?

text_blocks.snippet: To unfreeze an iPhone, perform a force restart, which safely reboots the device without erasing your data.
For most modern iPhones (iPhone 8 and newer):

list.snippet: Press and quickly release the Volume Up button.
Press and quickly release the Volume Down button.
Press and hold the Side (Power) button until the screen goes black and the Apple logo appears (usually takes 10–15 seconds), then release the button.

text_blocks.snippet: For a clear visual guide on exactly how fast you need to press the buttons to get your phone to restart:
If your specific model is older, the process varies slightly:

list.snippet: iPhone 7: Press and hold the Volume Down and Sleep/Wake buttons together until the Apple logo appears.
Older models with a Home button: Press and hold the Home and Sleep/Wake buttons simultaneously until the Apple logo appears.

text_blocks.snippet: If your phone still won't respond, charge it for at least 30 to 60 minutes and try again. For more detailed troubleshooting, check out the Apple Support Guide.
